entry.title: Automatically Remove Billing Blocks in Sales Order Billing Plans When Milestones Are Completed
entry.content:
1. Introduction

With the release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, milestone billing for sales orders becomes smarter and more automated.
Traditionally, sales order billing plans created from project milestones require manual removal of billing blocks as each milestone is completed. The new feature eliminates this manual step: when the project manager enters the actual finish date for a milestone, the system automatically removes the corresponding billing block in the sales order billing plan.
This enhancement allows project financial controllers, project managers, and internal sales representatives to collaborate more smoothly and ensures billing can proceed promptly.

 

2. Step-by-Step Process Overview

Step 1: Create a Revenue Project with Planned WBS and Milestones

Using the Project Control (F3215) or Project Planning (F3822) app, the project financial controller creates a revenue project, defines WBS elements, and plans milestones. A project manager is assigned to take ownership of project execution.

For example, four milestones may be planned under four billing elements. Each milestone corresponds to a specific billing percentage. As milestones are completed, the billing plan should progress toward 100%.

 

Step 2: Release the Project

Once planning is complete, the project financial controller or project manager releases the project so that execution can begin.

Step 3: Create a Sales Order with Milestone-Based Billing Plan

In the Manage Sales Orders (F3893) app, the internal sales representative creates a sales order based on the released revenue project.
To enable milestone billing, the item category must be CBAO – Milestone-Billing Plan.

After entering the WBS element at the item level, the user can generate a billing plan automatically by selecting Create from Milestone.

At creation time, the billing plan is automatically blocked, and each billing plan line is tied to a specific milestone.
The billing-related indicator “Used for Billing” on the WBS element is also updated to “Yes”.

Step 4: Enter Actual Finish Date for Milestones

When a milestone is reached, the project manager updates the Actual Finish Date for that milestone.
Upon entering the date, the system informs the user via a message strip that the related billing block will be removed automatically after the project is saved.

Step 5: Automatic Removal of Billing Block

After the project manager saves the update, the system runs an asynchronous background process that removes the billing block from the corresponding billing plan item.
The sales representative will then see that the billing block is cleared — allowing billing to continue without manual intervention.
This automation reduces administrative overhead and ensures timely billing aligned with project progress.

3. Summary

This feature enhances the milestone billing process by:

  • Eliminating manual steps to remove billing blocks
  • Improving collaboration between project and sales teams
  • Reducing billing delays and administrative workload
  • Ensuring billing plans always reflect up-to-date project status

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, milestone billing becomes more automated, accurate, and efficient—supporting smoother end-to-end project-to-cash operations.

entry.title: Rebase, Merge, Repeat: The Power of the Parallel Line
entry.content:
Co-Author: Disha

In today’s fast-paced business landscape, transformation doesn’t happen in a single wave. It happens in phases, across countries, business units, and multiple innovation cycles. SAP recognized this – and introduced the parallel line in SAP Central Business Configuration.

The parallel line is more than a second line. It’s a strategic enabler for structured, scalable, and low-risk digital transformation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

What Is the Parallel Line?

The parallel line allows you to run multiple configuration tracks in parallel within SAP Central Business Configuration – without disrupting the main implementation line.

Instead of being limited to a single linear workspace, you can now:

  • Run country rollouts in parallel
  • Extend scope and introduce new business processes after go-live without disrupting daily operations
  • Innovate safely while production remains stable

It brings enterprise-grade flexibility into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Switching to the parallel line

Why Parallel Line Is a Game Changer

The parallel line turns SAP Central Business Configuration into a transformation platform – not just a configuration tool. In the following sections, you’ll learn why.

Enables Global Rollouts with Confidence

Are you planning to roll out to a new country? Launch a new business unit? Introduce additional processes?

Large organizations rarely go live in all countries at once. With the parallel line, if your organization is rolling out operations to new legal entities or regions, you can extend scope and configure country-specific processes in a separate line.

  • A global template can go live in one country
  • Additional countries can be configured in parallel
  • Each rollout follows a structured governance path

Safe Space for Transformation

The parallel line allows you to create a copy of your existing workspace and carry out large or complex business configuration changes independently from your main line:

  • Introduce new modules over time
  • Expand functional scope step by step
  • Redesign organizational structures
  • Adjust operating models gradually

Your main line stays stable and focused, while innovation happens in parallel.

Test Before You Commit

One of the greatest strengths of the parallel line is the ability to configure and test thoroughly before merging changes into the main line. This structured approach reduces risk and increases implementation quality. Your productive landscape remains stable while you prepare for the next stage of growth.

Stay Aligned with Rebase

Business doesn’t stand still – and neither does your main line.

Once your parallel line is created, it can easily receive updates from the main line through a rebase operation. This keeps both lines synchronized and aligned, ensuring your workspace remains consistent and up to date.

You innovate in parallel, but you stay connected.

Options to rebase and merge the parallel line with the main line

Full Flexibility – Even to Start Over

If your strategy changes, you can delete the parallel line and start fresh (via SAP for Me). That level of flexibility ensures you remain in control throughout your transformation journey.

You’re never locked in – you’re always empowered.

How the Parallel Line and the Configuration Localization Tool Accelerate Localization

Example: Germany, Malta, and Cayman Islands

Imagine a company headquartered in Germany rolling out SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to Malta and the Cayman Islands.

Main Line (Germany HQ)

  • Chart of accounts
  • Core finance processes
  • Intercompany setup

Malta (Parallel Line)

  • 18% Value-Added Tax (VAT) setup and reduced rates
  • EU reverse charge
  • Local VAT returns & reporting formats

Cayman Islands (Parallel Line)

  • Local tax rules and reporting formats
  • Currency setup
  • Compliance with local regulatory requirements

How It Works

  1. The Global Template team configures core finance and procurement processes in the main line.
  2. The Malta and Cayman Islands teams configure local settings using the Configuration Localization Tool at the same time in the parallel line.
  3. Parallel testing ensures integration across countries, validates VAT calculations, and checks statutory reporting.
  4. The go-live can happen simultaneously or in planned waves, with confidence that the global template remains intact.

Key Outcomes

  • Faster rollout: You can configure and test multiple countries/regions in parallel.
  • Protected global template: Local changes for the countries localized with the Configuration Localization Tool don’t overwrite core configuration.
  • Early compliance validation: Intercompany and tax compliance issues can be detected sooner.
  • Scalable multi-country deployment: Ideal for companies expanding globally.
  • Better Resource Utilization: Teams work independently without waiting for others.

The parallel line keeps your core stable while you adapt, innovate, and localize faster. Simple, safe, scalable. Note that this feature is exclusively available in SAP Central Business Configuration with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3-system landscape.

Did you like this blog? If so, please leave us a thumbs up at the top. Also, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have any ideas on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the Customer Influence platform to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Automatic Batch Determination Using FEFO for Transfer Posting (Movement 311) in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Automatic Batch Determination Using FEFO for Transfer Posting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Introduction

  • In industries such as pharmaceuticals, food, chemicals, and FMCG, managing material shelf life is critical. Organizations must ensure that materials with the earliest expiration dates are consumed or transferred first to prevent wastage.
  • SAP provides a First Expiry First Out (FEFO) strategy through Batch Determination to automatically select batches based on their expiration dates.
  • This blog explains how to configure and use Automatic Batch Determination using FEFO for Transfer Posting (Movement Type 311) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Step 1 – Create Strategy Type

  • To enable batch determination for transfer posting, first create a Strategy Type for Inventory Management.

Menu Path

IMG  -> General Business Data ->  Batch Management -> Batch Determination -> Batch Determination for Inventory Management -> Define Inventory Management Strategy Types

Image 1-1

  • A new strategy type “ZB12” was created to enable FEFO-based batch determination.

       Image 1-2

Image 1-3

Step 2: Create Search Procedures

Image 2-1

  • A new search procedure “ZB0003” was created and the strategy type “ZB12” was assigned to it.

Image 2-2

Step 3- Allocate IM Search Procedure/Activate Check:

  • Assigned search procedure “ZB0003” to movement type 311.

Image 3-1

Step 4- Create Sort Sequence:

  • A new sort sequence “ZFEFO1” was created and the characteristic “LOBM_VFDAT” (Expiration Date) was assigned to ensure batches are sorted in ascending order based on the earliest expiry date, enabling the FEFO strategy.

Image 4-1

Image 4-2

Step 5- Create Class

  • Two classes were created: “ZBATCH_FEFO1” for assigning to the material in the classification view and “FEFOCLASS” for use in the batch search strategy.

Image 5-1: “ZBATCH_FEF0”

  • Assigned the class to material “FEFO2”.

Image 5-2:

Step 6: Manage Search Strategies – Inventory Management:

Image 6-1

  • In “Manage Search Strategies – Inventory Management”, the strategy was assigned for movement type 311 and class “FEFOCLASS” was maintained.

Image 6-2

Image 6-3

  • Assigned the sort sequence “ZFEFO1” to enable FEFO-based batch sorting.

Image 6-4

Step 7: Maintain Shelf Life Data in the Material Master

Scenario Testing:

Below is the screenshot of Stock Overview for the following:

Material: FEFO2

The stock overview displays batch-wise details including:

  • Production Date
  • SLED (Shelf Life Expiration Date)

This information helps the system determine the appropriate batch during FEFO-based batch determination, ensuring that batches with the earliest expiry date are selected first.

Image 8-1

Testing – Transfer of 10 Quantity

A stock transfer was executed:

  • Movement Type: 311
  • From Storage Location: 1001
  • To Storage Location: 1003
  • Quantity: 10

After clicking the Batch Determination icon:

  • The system automatically assigned the batch.
  • The batch selected was the one with the earliest expiry date, as per FEFO logic.
  • The expiry date was displayed during batch selection.

Image 8-2

Testing with Increased Quantity (20 Qty)

Further testing was performed by increasing the quantity to 20.

Since:

  • The first expiry batch “FEFO567” had only 10 quantity available,

After clicking the Batch Determination icon:

  • The system automatically allocated
  • 10 Qty from Batch FEFO567 (earliest expiry batch)
  • Remaining quantity from the next earliest expiry batch

This confirms that the system splits the quantity automatically based on FEFO logic.

Conclusion:

The FEFO concept is functioning as expected during stock transfer using movement type 311.

  • Batch determination correctly selects the batch with the earliest expiry date based on the FEFO principle.
  • Expired batches are not considered during batch determination when the remaining shelf life condition is maintained.
  • The system automatically splits quantities across multiple batches when the required quantity exceeds the available stock in the first batch.

entry.title: Exploring Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 (HFC6)
entry.content:
I'm excited to share the latest highlights for Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 (HFC6). This release brings significant enhancements to Manufacturing and Production Planning, designed to provide production planners, supervisors, and supply chain managers with greater control, visibility, and efficiency. Lets explore the new capabilities that will help you streamline your operations.


Manage Material Coverage - Shipping Notification Quick View

In the Manage Material Coverage app (F0251A), we have introduced a new quick view for the MRP element Shipping Notification (ShpgNt). This enhancement provides planners with immediate access to critical information without navigating away from their main screen. The quick view displays details about the supplier, the responsible internal contact, and the confirmation status received from the supplier.

Manage Material Coverage - Shipping Notification Quick View

For deeper analysis, you can now navigate directly to the related purchase order from this view, significantly speeding up issue resolution and improving situational awareness.

 

Improved Stock Transfer Between MRP Areas

The overall stock transfer process between MRP areas is seen below. 

Stock Transfer Between MRP Areas

For what business use case do you need this new scenario?

The business case is that this new process allows you to have several storage location MRP areas (warehouses) now that are supplying your production warehouse.

Why do you need several source storage locations (warehouses)?

One of the reasons might be that you might want to have your supplier deliver raw materials or components to these source storage locations first, and then do a stock transfer from these source based locations to your production site or to your main warehouse in the production facility.  Meaning in SAP language that the raw materials or components that are stored in the source warehouses (source MRP area) are now needed in the target production facility (target MRP area). Also, another reason might be that you want to line up more than one source MRP area and then in the target MRP area you do the final assembly. 

Now with the latest release, the MRP can be used for the stock transfer of components from the warehouse (source MRP area) storage location to the production storage location (target MRP area) by either a using a stock transfer reservation or requisition. The next example is for a stock transfer reservation: 

Example: Stock Transfer Reservation

In the Manage Material Coverage you can see the result from the reservation for the target MRP area: 

Example: Stock transfer reservation

Based on the supply in the target MRP area, the MRP creates the demand in the source MRP area via stock transfer reservation: 

Example: Stock Transfer Requisition

This scenario also works if source MRP area and target MRP area belong to different plants. The target MRP area can also be used as a subcontracting MRP area. 

This feature improves the value of independent MRP areas and minimizes master data maintenance efforts, streamlining the planning process.


Capacity Scheduling Board

We have improved the user experience within the Capacity Scheduling Board. For better visibility and context during scheduling, you can now display the Product ID and Product Description directly in the Gantt chart. Simply access the Gantt Settings and select the Product ID and Product Description column to add it to your view:

View settings: Product ID and Product Description

 The result after selecting the above named settings looks like the following: 

Product ID and Product Description included as information

This removes the need to look up product details separately, making the scheduling process faster and more intuitive for your planners.

Manage Work Center Capacity

Another highlight is in the app Manage Work Center Capacity (F3289). Here we added the capability in the overview section to select the utilization with Maintenance as seen in the following screen: 

Utilization with Maintenance Settings

This gives the production planner not only the view of the utilization for planned or production orders, but also for maintenance orders to improve capacity planning on the respective Work Centers. 

Also, we included the maintenance order capacity information (in % and hours) in the table overview, so that the production planner can also use the grid view: 

Table with Maintenance Capacity View

 With this addition the maintenance order capacity value is now included in the utilization chart of the Manage Work Center Capacity Fiori app to give a holistic planning view to the production planner for their day to day work. 

SAP Cloud ERP: Manufacturing | Demos - How Tos 

Last but not least, I would like to introduce our new SAP Cloud ERP: Manufacturing  channel on YouTube to you. This new channel is embedded into the SAP Help and Learning YouTube channel which is your go-to destination for tutorials, product demos, and expert guidance on SAP solutions. Whether you’re getting started or looking to deepen your expertise, our videos cover setup, best practices, and step-by-step walkthroughs to help you succeed. If you have any topics where we need to create a Microlearning, please let us know. 

That's it and stay tuned - the next HFC is just around the corner. 

Best regards, 

Markus

entry.title: Video Series for Customers Converting their SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2SL to 3SL
entry.content:
We’re pleased to introduce a new series of concise, practical videos designed to support customers transitioning from a 2‑system to a 3‑system landscape in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The videos highlight the benefits, key considerations, and what to expect during the conversion journey.

Here you can find the links to the videos:

  • Managing Transports in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape: This video describes the transport management processes for different types of data from Development, to Test, to Production. The transport process is organized, streamlined, and reduces risk when using the 3-System landscape.
  • Exploring the Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape: This video describes the features of the Test Automation Tool to simplify business process testing after release upgrades. The Test Automation Tool can also be used when a Parallel Line is generated to create a secondary test tenant. Customers receive the most value from the Test Automation Tool when used in a 3-System landscape.
  • Navigating Parallel Line in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape: This video describes the features of the Parallel Line, which can be generated as a copy of your main line to provide an additional SAP Central Business Configuration workspace, Development tenant, and Test tenant for a variety of different use cases. This feature has no cost and is only available in 3-System landscape.
  • Exploring Developer Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape: This video highlights three different types of extensibility: Key User, Developer, and Side-by-Side. While Key User and Side-by-Side extensibility are available in both 2 and 3-System landscape, Developer extensibility is only available in 3-System landscape. Developer extensibility provides a unique opportunity to create customer-specific extensions to released SAP objects with ABAP code, providing a deeper layer of customization.
  • Using the Configuration Localization Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape: This video describes the features of the Configuration Localization Tool to create customer-specific countries, which is only available in 3-System landscape.

If you want to learn how to implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and get certified, please refer to this learning journey: Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Depersonalization in Test Data Refresh – effects on Procurement Teams
entry.content:
During the performance of a Test Data Refresh (TDR) in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud, SAP offers three different levels of depersonalization:

Source: Depersonalization Feature | SAP Help Portal

 

In this blog, I want to highlight the effects of the choice of depersonalization mode on Procurement Teams.

 

Background Info:

In the App Manage Teams and Responsibilities – Procurement you can manage teams, responsibility definitions, and team member functions. You can use responsibility definitions and member functions to determine members (recipients) that are responsible for workflows, business scenarios, and situation handling. See Manage Teams and Responsibilities | SAP Help Portal for more info.

 

Effects on Procurement Teams:

When performing a TDR, a customer will have to decide on one of the three depersonalization mode. So how does this decision affect Procurement Teams?

 

An improvement request to prevent the necessity of changes when choosing Depersonalization On or Depersonalization Level 1 has been raised and is currently being investigated by SAP: Improvement Request 351502 - Retention of team members after Test Data Refresh.

So, in the future, there might be changes in this behaviour. But for now, from a procurement perspective it is necessary to pay close attention to the depersonalization mode.

 

Summary:

  • When choosing Depersonalization On or Depersonalization Level 1 the team owners and team members will be changed during TDR and need to manually be changed again afterwards.
  • Choosing Depersonalization On or Depersonalization Level 1 poses the risk of sending approval tasks to wrong employees.
  • When working with Depersonalization On or Depersonalization Level 1 it helps to turn off all workflows before performing a TDR.

 

Relevant sources to bookmark to find out about any updates:

  • 3070606 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Test Data Refresh (TDR) - Information Note - SAP for Me
  • Test Data Refresh | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: All Finance enablement materials are refreshed to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602
entry.content:
What’s new and why it matters

  • All Finance enablement content has been reviewed and updated to reflect the functional and UX changes in release 2602.
  • Most courses include a sandbox environment so you can practise end-to-end processes and validate new features hands-on.
  • Learning journeys group relevant courses so you can follow a structured path from overview to deep-dive and configuration topics.
  • Each course is listed with its course code so you can quickly search and enroll in the SAP training portal or your corporate learning platform.

Learning journeys and included courses, with available sandbox system (or coming up):

Financial Accounting

Outlining the Financial Accounting Overview

  • F1111 — Outlining Processes in Payables Management (with sandbox system)
  • F1112 — Outlining Processes in Receivables Management (with sandbox system)
  • F1113 — Outlining Processes in Record-to-Report
  • F1114 — Outlining Processes in Asset Accounting (with sandbox system)

Outlining the Record-to-Report Process

  • F1120 — Explaining Record-to-Report Processes (Classroom Training)
  • F1121 — Explaining the Master Data Concept in Record-to-Report (with sandbox system)
  • F1122 — Explaining General Concepts of G/L Postings (with sandbox system)
  • F1123 — Explaining Postings to Meet Reporting Requirements (with sandbox system)

Describing the Receivables Management Process

F1151 — Explaining Asset Accounting Processes (with sandbox system + Classroom Training)

Designing the Record-to-Report Process

  • F1211 — Detailing Accruals Management (with sandbox system)

  • F1213 — Detailing Close Orchestration with SAP Advanced Financial Closing

Using the Payables Management Process

  • F1221 — Detailing Down Payments Execution (with sandbox system)
  • F1222 — Detailing the Configuration for the Payment Run
  • F1223 — Detailing Process Automation in Accounts Payable (with sandbox system)

Operating the Receivables Management Process

  • F1231 — Detailing Special G/L Transactions in Accounts Receivable (with sandbox system)
  • F1232 — Detailing Digital Payments Collaboration
  • F1233 — Detailing SAP Cash Application and Processing Rules For Bank Statements
  • F1234 — Detailing Dispute and Collections Management
  • F1235 — Detailing Credit Management Processes in Accounts Receivable
  • F1236 — Detailing the Dunning Run (with sandbox system)

Designing the Asset Accounting Process

  • F1240 — Designing the Asset Accounting Process (Classroom Training)
  • F1241 — Detailing Settings for Asset Accounting (with sandbox system)
  • F1242 — Detailing Asset Accounting Integration Aspects (with sandbox system)
  • F1243 — Detailing Asset Accounting Year-end closing activities (with sandbox system)

Configuring the Record-to-Report Process

  • F1251 — Detailing Configuration of R2R Posting Processes (with sandbox system)
  • F1252 — Detailing Advanced Valuation
  • F1253 — Detailing Balance Validation (with sandbox system)

F1261 — Detailing Profit Center Reorganization

Management Accounting

  • F2111 — Outlining Cost Management and Profitability Analysis
  • F12T0 — Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Academy (with sandbox system + Classroom Training)
  • F21T0 — Explaining Business Processes in Management Accounting  (Classroom Training)
  • F22T0 — Implementing Management Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Academy (with sandbox system)

Performing Overhead Cost Controlling

  • F2121 — Explaining Cost Center Accounting (with sandbox system)
  • F2122 — Explaining Project Controlling (with sandbox system)

Product Cost / Production Accounting

  • F2161 — Outlining Cost Estimate Options in Product Cost Planning

F2211 — Detailing Overhead Cost Accounting (with sandbox system)

F2231 — Detailing Profitability Accounting for Discrete Industries (with sandbox system)

Evaluating Production Accounting in Make-to-Stock Scenarios

Real Estate

Group Reporting / Corporate Close

  • F9212 — Explaining Corporate Close with Group Reporting (with sandbox system)

entry.title: BKP – Alternative Project Solution for Classical Intercompany Returns (2F4)
entry.content:
Context

You have used intercompany sales solution for your business, corespondingly, returns intercompany solution is needed.

Case 1: In sales, if the classical intercompany sales (scope item 1MX, or 1HO) is used, classical intercompany returns (scope item 2F4) should be the first solution to handle corresponding intercompany returns.

Case 2: In sales, if the advanced intercompany sales (scope item 5D2) is used, classical intercompany returns (scope item 2F4) would be a good short-term solution to handle corresponding intercompany returns, if applicable (5W2 group valuation is not activated). Check more other potentials at BKP - Customer Returns Project Solution for Advanced Intercompany Sales

For classical intercompany returns (2F4), it handles direct shipment of returned product. The solution is compact, integrated, and can fulfill your regular business needs.  

Meanwhile, if you have below special business needs, 2F4 solution can’t handle them well. In short term, there is no enhancement considered.

Case 1: Finance or legal requirement: During goods receipt, returned product should not be valuated (meaning, do not take it back), valuation should take place later such as the logistic follow up decision made during inspection. This is not supported. With solution 2F4, returned product is automatically valuated during post goods receipt in receiving company and the sales cost is reversed. This is by design.

Case 2: Internal credit memo when material inspection split happens. This is not supported. If there is material inspection split for one return item, it is not possible to trigger the internal credit memo creation. System gives an error and blocks the creation. This is the function gap today.

If the above two issues are critical in your business, you can consider an alternative project solution to deal with your intercompany returns business. Customer Returns (BKP) gives your enough process and implementation flexibility.

Standard Solution: 2F4 Classical intercompany returns  

In general, the classical intercompany returns process is a simplified, out of box solution. Step by step guidelines are available in scope item 2F4 .

Classic Intercompany Returns - Single Level / Single System

Below is the classical intercompany returns process flow. This is available in returns overview at item level in manage customer returns version 2 App.

Alternative Solution - Using Logistical Follow-Up Activity (LFU) 0007 – Directly ship to supplier

In general, this is a customized project solution based on customer returns (BKP)

In this solution, basically, we split the returns process for selling company and delivering company. Each company runs BKP (customer returns) separately. It is not one integrated solution.  We utilize the predefined logistical follow-up activity (LFU) 0007 “directly ship to supplier” in the selling company.  This solution option has the following highlights:

  • We establish an internal customer (selling company) and internal supplier (delivering company) relationship - Treating delivering company as the supplier for selling company. 
  • In this return’s scenario, product is returned to delivering plant (delivering company) directly, this is the same as solution in 2F4 classical intercompany returns.
  • Enhanced document flow - returns purchase order and supplier credit memo in selling company, and intercompany returns order in delivering company are created.
  • Inventory, sales cost and revenue can be reversed with regular finance accounting in the two companies.

Selling company handles the returns from the end customer as follows:

  1. Create a returns order with reference as usual or without reference. Use logistical follow-up activity 0007 “Direct ship to supplier”, input the business partner: BP of the delivery company code (not the delivery plant) as supplier, select the right return-to address from the identified supplier. Change the plant to the plant of selling company, instead of the copied intercompany plant.
  2. No returns delivery to the selling company – the customer sends the goods to delivery company directly.
  3. A returns purchase order for the delivering company is created following step 1.
  4. Receive a credit memo from the delivering company and perform supplier credit memo verification accordingly.
  5. Determine refund for the end customer with credit memo request and credit memo.

Delivering company handles (internal) customer returns from selling company as follows:

  1. Create a returns order manually to handle (intercompany) customer returns using the BP of selling company as the sold-to party. Creation with reference is not possible. You should use refund control – decide later if you want to skip the product valuation during goods receipt.
  2. The returns delivery is created, and post goods receipt once the end customer ships back the product. As this is the regular customer returns (BKP) – receive into plant scenario for delivering company, by default, the received product is non-valuated. There is no finance posting during post goods receipt.
  3. Perform product inspection and decide the final logistical follow-up as usual. Material inspection split can be applied here.
  4. Determine the refund for the internal customer – selling company with credit memo request and credit memo.  If there is material inspection split, it is also supported to have split refund in manage customer returns version 2 App.

Master Data Set Up for the Alternative Solution

There are some tips to highlight for the alternative solution. 

  • Use the BP of delivery company code as the supplier. Do not use the BP of the delivering plant.
  • For supplier master data, ensure the returns supplier is selected. With selection, you must assign a customer (same partner with customer role) to deal with the outbound shipping for returns purchase order.

  • You might maintain the right return–to address for the supplier.  Check the help document Return-to Address Determination for Supplier Returns
  •  Ensure you have the right info record maintained for the returns purchase order.

Summary

2F4 classical intercompany returns can be a first consideration as it is compact, integrated, out of box solution, if it fulfills the regular business.

If product valuation should be avoided during goods receipt, or material inspection split happens often, then the alternative project solution with LFU 0007 – Directly ship to supplier would be recommended.

entry.title: Agentic AI Gains Momentum as Partners Build Content‑ and Code‑Based Customer‑Driven Solutions
entry.content:
This edition of Hack2Build – Custom AI Solutions brought together partners who are already building agentic AI solutions aligned to real customer needs. Reflecting SAP’s dual‑path approach to agent development, the edition supported both content‑based agents built with Agent Builder in Joule Studio, and code‑based agents developed with the Joule Studio code editor and SAP Cloud SDK for AI on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).

Hac2Build Custom AI Solutions March 10-17, 2026

 

Across a focused 7‑day hack phase, followed by a 5‑hour Demo Jam on March 17, 13 partner teams delivered functional prototypes demonstrating how agentic AI can drive efficiency, improve decisions, and transform enterprise workflows across procurement, finance, tax, supply chain, utilities, and sales.

Hack2Build: Custom AI Solutions Edition

Throughout the program, partners worked hands‑on with SAP’s agentic AI stack, including:

  • Agent Builder in Joule Studio for rapid design of content‑grounded agents
  • Joule Studio code editor for pro‑code extensibility, tool creation, and MCP integrations
  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI to integrate LLMs and orchestrate models via SAP AI Core
  • Generative AI Hub for model access, prompt lifecycle, and multi‑model orchestration
  • SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine & Knowledge Graph for semantic search and relationship‑aware reasoning
  • SAP Build Process Automation for human approvals, workflow steps, and document handling

This allowed teams to combine reasoning, retrieval, decision logic, and system actions into autonomous workflows built directly on SAP BTP.

Partners selected to participate in the second edition of Hack2Build Custom AI Solutions, March 10-17, 2026

 
After a week of rapid design and iteration, the Demo Jam brought all 13 partner solutions together in a fast‑paced, five‑hour showcase. Each team presented a fully working agent built around a concrete customer scenario, demonstrating how quickly agentic workflows can be applied to real business processes. 

🏆Winners 

With that momentum, the judges selected three solutions that stood out across technical completeness, workflow clarity, and customer relevance.

🥇Capgemini 

Capgemini’s Demand‑to‑Award Agent is an end‑to‑end agentic workflow built on SAP BTP that automates the procurement lifecycle from initial demand intake through supplier award. Incoming requests from email and Excel are processed using using SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Document AI, then enriched with SAP S/4HANA master data. A Demand Management agent classifies requests into Immediate and Projection categories by evaluating delivery timelines, MRP signals, historical demand patterns, and policy rules. Developed using a blend of low‑code agents in Agent Builder in Joule Studio and pro‑code logic, and supported by SAP Build Process Automation and RAG capabilities in SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine. Downstream agents orchestrate supplier shortlisting, RFQ execution, quotation evaluation, awarding, and purchase‑order creation directly in SAP S/4HANA.  

Capgemini 's Demand‑to‑Award Procurement Agent

🥈DataXstream 

DataXstream’s Churn Risk Agent is a retention‑workflow solution on SAP BTP for sales and account teams using the DataXstream's OMS+ SAP front end. The agent evaluates churn indicators, recommends next‑best retention actions, retrieves promotional pricing and material availability, and prepares a draft customer response—all within a single guided flow. Powered by Joule and containerized ML services deployed on SAP BTP, Kyma runtime and OMS+ integration, the solution consolidates fragmented churn signals into prioritized, actionable guidance tailored for customer‑facing users. DataXstream 's Churn Risk Agent

🥉OpenText 

OpenText’s Cognitive P2P Advisor is an orchestrator agent on SAP BTP supporting invoice‑exception resolution to streamline the procure‑to‑pay (P2P) process. It merges structured data from SAP S/4HANA with unstructured documents stored in OpenText Core Archive to provide contextual insights for each exception. Using the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph to interpret relationships and hierarchical dependencies, the agent blends graph‑based reasoning with semantic document understanding. The result is a set of structured resolution proposals that guide users through root‑cause diagnosis and remediation in P2P workflows. OpenText's Cognitive P2P Advisor

⭐ Finalists

Beyond the winning solutions, the enthusiasm and creativity shown by the remaining finalists reflected the strength of SAP’s partner ecosystem. Across industries such as finance, supply chain, utilities, sales, and tax, partners delivered content‑ and code‑based agents with clear business relevance and strong potential for rapid customer adoption. Their prototypes demonstrated how agentic AI can be applied to diverse operational workflows, proving that partners are already translating customer needs into practical, scalable agent solutions on SAP BTP.

PartnerAgentic AI Solution
AyesaUtilities Switching Agent
CapgeminiDemand‑to‑Award Procurement Agent
DataRobotOptimization Planning Agent
DataXstreamChurn Risk Agent
DeloitteP&L Advisor – Finance
InflexionBid Advisor
KPMGIntelligent Data Harmonization Agent
MouriTechIntelligent Month-End Close Agent
OpenTextCognitive P2P Advisor
RSG Red SinergiaTax Compliance Assistant with SAP AI
Semos CloudAI SDR Pipeline Agent
TCSAP ResolveX
Thomson ReutersTransaction Tax Analysis and Anomaly Detection Agent

These solutions addressed real‑world customer challenges—from procurement and finance to utilities and tax—demonstrating the range and adaptability of SAP’s agentic development model.

What’s Next

All participating partners now move into a four‑week Build Phase, where they will refine and extend their prototypes with support from SAP product engineering, Joule Studio teams, SAP AI experts, and Partner Ecosystem Success. This phase will focus on strengthening grounding strategies, tool calling, integrations, human‑in‑the‑loop flows, and preparing solutions for customer validation and potential go‑to‑market discussions.

Acknowledgments

Thank you to all partner teams, Judges, and SAP contributors across Joule Studio, Business AI Product Engineering and Regional Implementation Group,   BTP and AI Solution Architects, Partner Excellence Center Leads and Partner Ecosystem Success for making this edition of Hack2Build a milestone in advancing partner‑built agentic AI.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition：高级外币评估中的跨币种清账处理
entry.content:
业务背景

本篇博客旨在说明和探讨SAP近年推出的高级外币评估功能如何处理实际业务中可能发生的跨币种清账业务。

存在跨币种清账数据的场景下，由于交易货币 (00) 可能不同于公司代码货币 (10) 和集团货币 (30)，在发票凭证被收付款清算后，相关已清项可能还会列示在高级外币评估中，生成外币评估分录，以体现汇率变动对外币业务数据的影响。

 

系统测试

测试用例前提条件：公司代码货币CNY；集团货币USD，且设置集团货币由公司代码货币换算得出。

测试系统的货币汇率设置如下：

 

示例1：

交易货币层面：应付账款 10,000 USD，付款清账 69,755.00 CNY，跨月清账

 

发票凭证：

应付账款 10,000 USD，过账日期 2025-12-28，USD/CNY = 6.33745

 

付款凭证：

付款清账69,755.00 CNY，过账日期 2026-01-20，USD/CNY = 6.97550

 

期间1执行高级外币评估：

分析关账运行结果（F3753），运行日期：2025-12-31，USD/CNY = 6.97550

测试运行

正式运行

 

查看期间1生成的日记账分录：

 

期间2执行高级外币评估：

分析关账运行结果（F3753），运行日期：2026-01-31，USD/CNY = 6.90410

测试运行

正式运行

应付账款10,000 USD，基于货币汇率 USD/CNY = 6.33745 得出公司代码货币金额 = 63,375.40 CNY；同时得出集团货币金额 = 10,000 USD。

付款清账 69,755.00 CNY，基于货币汇率 USD/CNY = 6.97550 得出集团货币金额 = 10,000 USD。

上述两笔分录在公司代码金额层面相差 6,380 CNY，由于在公司代码货币层面存在汇兑损益差额，所以列示在高级外币评估中 (如上图)。

 

查看期间2生成的日记账分录：

按照不同货币类型查看日记账分录：

 

业务凭证流梳理如下：

 

示例2：

交易货币：应付账款 10,000 EUR，付款清账 12,379.44 USD，当月清账

 

发票凭证：

应付账款 10,000 EUR，过账日期2025-12-28，EUR/CNY = 7.84541，USD/CNY = 6.33745

 

付款凭证：

付款清账 12,379.44 USD，过账日期 2025-12-29，USD/CNY = 6.33745

 

执行高级外币评估：

分析关账运行结果（F3753），运行日期：2025-12-31，EUR/CNY = 8.34360，USD/CNY = 6.97550

测试运行

正式运行

应付账款10,000 EUR，基于货币汇率 EUR/CNY = 7.84541 得出公司代码货币金额 = 78,454.10 CNY；基于货币汇率 USD/CNY = 6.33745得出集团货币金额 = 12,379.44 USD。

付款清账 12,379.44 USD，基于货币汇率 USD/CNY = 6.33745 得出公司代码货币金额 = 78,454.08 CNY；同时得出集团货币金额 = 12,379.44 USD。

上述两笔分录在公司代码金额层面相差 0.02 CNY，由于在公司代码货币层面存在汇兑损益差额，所以列示在高级外币评估中 (如上图)。

 

查看高级外币评估生成的日记账分录：

 

业务凭证流梳理如下：

 

总结

从上述示例中可以看出，汇率差异体现在公司代码货币 (10) 或者集团货币 (30) 下，会通过高级外币评估功能生成日记账分录，借贷源科目（原始统驭科目）以体现汇率波动的影响，但调整的原始统驭科目是S类型总账科目，不会影响客户或者供应商实际未清项的清算数据。

综上所述，SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition系统中的高级外币评估功能能够处理跨币种清账业务，在每个期间，按照期末汇率过账汇兑损益重估分录，以体现汇率波动对于此类业务的影响。

entry.title: How to Achieve Storage Location-Level Planning in SAP Cloud ERP - Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction

Material Requirements Planning (MRP) plays a critical role in ensuring material availability while optimizing inventory levels. In traditional SAP systems, businesses often leveraged storage location-level MRP for granular planning. However, in SAP Cloud ERP, storage location-level MRP is not directly supported.

To address this limitation, organizations can use MRP Areas to achieve similar functionality. This blog explains how MRP Areas—specifically storage location MRP Areas—can be used as an effective alternative.

Things to Know

What is MRP?

MRP (Material Requirements Planning) is a planning tool used to ensure that the right materials are available at the right time and in the right quantity. It considers demand, current stock, and procurement proposals.

Plant-Level MRP

At the plant level, MRP plans materials considering the entire plant stock. It does not differentiate between storage locations, which may lead to inefficiencies when materials are managed differently across locations.

Storage Location-Level MRP

In legacy SAP systems, this allowed planning at a more granular level, enabling businesses to plan materials separately for each storage location.
In SAP Cloud ERP, this functionality is not directly available.

What is an MRP Area?

MRP Area is an organizational unit that allows MRP to be executed for:

  • A whole plant
  • A specific storage location
  • A subcontractor

By defining storage locations as MRP Areas, you can simulate storage location-level planning.

Business Scenario

Consider a manufacturing company operating on SAP Cloud ERP, Public Cloud, where the same raw material is stored in two different locations within a single plant—one close to the production line and the other in a central warehouse. For instance, bolts are kept in a production storage location for daily shop floor consumption and in a warehouse storage location for bulk storage and backup supply. The production area requires frequent replenishment in smaller quantities, whereas the warehouse manages larger volumes with less frequent planning cycles.

When MRP is executed at the plant level, the system aggregates stock from both locations and plans collectively. This can result in misleading availability, where overall stock appears sufficient, but the production location may still experience shortages.

To address this, the company aims to plan material requirements separately for each storage location—ensuring uninterrupted supply to production while maintaining optimal inventory in the warehouse.

This scenario can be effectively handled in SAP Cloud ERP, Public Cloud by creating MRP Areas of type 02 (storage location) for each location, enabling independent planning and better inventory control.

Now, Let’s deep dive into how to set up storage location MRP Area and how it impacts the MRP run.

Configuration Steps

Define MRP Areas  under the configuration customization node Material Requirement Planning (MRP) > Define MRP Area for Plant/Storage Locations

Add a new entry to create MRP Area (ZSLOCWP) for the storage location 1 (here it is SLoc – 118S) and make sure the MRP Area Type is 02

In the details screen, Enter the MRP Area description, Receiving Storage location (If more than one storage locations assigned to the MRP area, then the receiving storage location will be the one to which all the components are received), enable the checkbox ‘Assign Materials automatically’ and fill in the MRP Area Profile as SMRPTYPE_PD (if the specified storage location needs to plan separately) or SMRPTYPE_ND (if the specified storage location needs to be excluded from planning). In this blog we are considering the former scenario (i.e. to plan the specified storage location separately)

Under the tab ‘Assign storage locations,’ add storage locations that are needs to be planned separately from the Plant level MRP

Navigate to the Fiori app ‘Change material master’ (tcode: MM02) > under MRP1 View > Under the section MRP Area, click the button ‘MRP Areas’ > Add the newly created MRP Area ‘ZSLOCWP’ for storage location 118S and make sure that material is extended to the same storage location.

In the details of MRP Area, enter the MRP Type, MRP Controller (which acts as a filter while scheduling MRP Run only specific to that MRP Area), Lot Size Procedure and then click on Adopt

Similar to the above steps creating MRP Area ZSLOCWP1 for another storage location (let’s say 118F) in the customization configuration screen.

In the details screen, provide the description, receiving storage location and MRP Area Profile as SMRPTYPE_PD to plan separately for the specified location. If more than one storage locations assigned to the MRP area, then the receiving storage location will be the one to which all the components are received.

Here assigns the other storage location 118F that needs to be planned separately from other locations/plant.

Navigate to MM02 > Add the secondly created MRP Area ‘ZSLOCWP1’ and make sure that the material is extended to the storage location for which the MRP area is created.

Fill in the mandatory fields for MRP Area 

Production Planning Steps

Goto MD61 (Create Planned Independent Requirement) > maintain the requirement quantity for the material at MRP area level and make sure that version is active to considered it for MRP run. Here, maintained 100EA for the month of March 2026 and April 2026 for both the storage location level MRP Area and Plant MRP Area.

Navigate to the Fiori app ‘Monitor Material Coverage Net/Individual segments’ > Filter with the material and plant number > select the displayed line item and click on Manage Materials option at the top of the list. It displays the IndReq created ate MRP Area level .

Goto Fiori app ‘Schedule MRP Runs’ > fill in the required parameters which includes, Plant, Material, MRP Controller (if required). Though we don’t have separate field for MRP Area, MRP live considers all the MRP Areas defined in the plant for the specified material and performs planning at each MRP Area level. Once all the data are entered , click on ‘Schedule’. Job will be scheduled at the backend.

Once the job got finished, Job log can be viewed by clicking on the files icon in the Results column.

In the below screen, it is visible that the MRP Live considered all the MRP Area for planning and planned each of them separately. Hence created separated line item for each of the MRP Areas.

For the MRP Area ZSLOCWP (storage location 118S), it created 2 Planned orders for the current month with quantity 85EA (as there already exists some stock in the location 118S) and the other with 100EA for the next month.

For the MRP Area ZSLOCWP1 (storage location 118F), it created 2 Planned orders for both the months each with quantity 100EA (as there no stock exists in the location 118F). It considered only that storage location stock and not the existing stock in the plant.

For the Plant MRP Area 1811, it created 2 Planned orders for both the months with quantity 100EA and it didn’t considered the stock in the storage location MRP Area.

Conclusion

Although storage location-level MRP is not directly supported in SAP Cloud ERP, MRP Areas provide a robust and flexible alternative. By leveraging storage location MRP Areas, businesses can achieve granular planning, better inventory control, and improved operational efficiency.

Hope this blog helps you to better understand how to effectively use MRP Areas to achieve storage location-level planning in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. If you have any questions, feedback, or would like to share how you are implementing this in your organization, feel free to drop your comments below.

Happy Learning!! 🙂

entry.title: Quality Gates: Leveraging the continuous quality check (CQC) Service Report for Project Readiness
entry.content:
Quality Gates are a core component of the SAP Activate methodology, designed to ensure projects move forward in a structured, controlled, and high-quality way. Each Quality Gate serves as a formal checkpoint at the end of a phase, confirming that the project is ready to transition to the next phase with the right foundations in place.

What brings consistency, scalability, and real insight into this process, particularly for SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors, is the continuous quality check (CQC) for implementation gates service report. Rather than relying on manual validation alone, the CQC service report enables a standardized, data-driven assessment of project readiness, ensuring alignment with SAP best practices and SAP Activate methodology.  

Within SAP Cloud ALM, the Quality Gate feature is used to:

  • Conduct the associated Quality Gate
  • Submit checklist responses for evaluation
  • Request the CQC for implementation gates service report (only for Prepare and Explore phases)

Once submitted, the CQC service report is automatically generated based on the checklist inputs. It delivers tailored recommendations specific to your project.

The results are fully integrated within SAP Cloud ALM:

  • CQC service results
  • Downloadable PDF report
  • Standalone action items

All of these are accessible within the Service pillar, providing a centralized location for managing quality insights and follow-up actions.

The CQC service report is a structured output generated as part of the Quality Gate service. It consolidates:

  • Responses to Quality Gate questions
  • Identified risks, gaps, and recommendations
  • A summarized readiness assessment

It serves as a single source of truth for the Quality Gate evaluation, particularly for the Prepare and Explore phases, and supports the decision on whether the project is ready to proceed to the next phase.

The CQC service report enhances the process by:

Standardizing the Assessment

Ensures all projects are evaluated using consistent SAP-defined criteria.

Ensuring Alignment to Best Practices

Validates that the project adheres to SAP Activate methodology and recommended implementation standards.

Identifying Risks Early

Highlights gaps and risks that could impact downtime phases if not addressed.

The report is designed to deliver value to both executive stakeholders and the project team, typically including:

  • Key Findings: A concise summary of the most important insights for stakeholders.
    • Positive Findings: Areas where SAP best practices are being successfully applied.
    • Significant Findings: Issues requiring remediation or course correction.
    • Critical Findings: Critical issues requiring remediation or course correction.
  • Action Plan: Prioritized actions with a clear priority and deadlines.
  • Assessment Results: Detailed responses to Quality Gate questions.

The Conduct Quality Gate task is no longer just a milestone, it is an opportunity to validate, align, and strengthen your project using real insights. By leveraging the CQC service report within SAP Cloud ALM, project teams can ensure they are not only completing required activities but doing so in alignment with SAP best practices. This enables a confident transition into the next phase, aligned, prepared, and set up for success.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council-Sales: Driving Better Collaboration and Product Usability
entry.content:
SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council
Making collaboration more direct. Making products more usable.

As more customers adopt SAP Public Cloud, partners play an increasingly critical role in sales, delivery, and customer success.

At the same time, we have consistently heard feedback from our partners:

  • The need for earlier visibility into SAP’s roadmap
  • Better clarity on what capabilities will be delivered and when
  • A more direct way to bring real project requirements into product discussions

To address these needs, SAP Public Cloud Sales has launched the SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council (PAC).

Why PAC?

Traditionally, partner feedback has been collected through channels such as WCR and CIP.

While these mechanisms are important for structured feedback, we have observed some limitations:

  • Feedback cycles can be relatively long, making it difficult to respond quickly to real project needs
  • Inputs are often based on individual requirements rather than broader business scenarios
  • The process of how feedback influences the product roadmap is not always transparent

PAC is designed to complement these channels by providing a more direct and interactive way to collaborate.

Our goal is simple: enable faster communication, clearer alignment, and improved product usability.

What we do in PAC

PAC focuses on real product topics and real project challenges.

Roadmap sharing
We share the latest roadmap updates for SAP Public Cloud (e.g. S/4HANA Cloud, BTP), including planned and under-evaluation capabilities, and explain their impact on sales and project delivery.

Real-time discussion and Q&A
We collect partner questions in advance, discuss key topics during the session, and define clear follow-up actions where needed.

Direct connection with product teams
PAC enables direct interaction between partners and product teams, helping ensure that real business scenarios are better reflected in product design.

Real use cases and experience sharing
Partners are encouraged to share project experiences, integration and extensibility scenarios, as well as sales and delivery insights.

What we have discussed so far

PAC sessions are built around real agendas.

Topics from previous sessions include:

  • SAP Cloud ERP for Sales strategy and roadmap
  • Fiori roadmap (e.g. 2602 / 2608) with system demos
  • Manage Sales Documents scenarios
  • Extensibility and UI use cases
  • Custom Sales Order Type
  • Partner Success Stories and Customer Stories
  • Product surveys, user research, and partner feedback

Each session also includes dedicated time for open discussion, Q&A, and clear next steps.

A continuously evolving format

PAC is not a one-time event.

We continuously shape future PAC sessions based on the topics partners are most interested in. This ensures that discussions remain relevant and that feedback can effectively influence product improvements.

What partners gain

By participating in PAC, partners can:

  • Better understand product direction and roadmap
  • Reduce uncertainty in sales and project delivery
  • Bring requirements more directly into product discussions
  • Learn from other partners’ real-world experiences

How to join

The SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council is invitation-based.

To ensure open and productive discussions, participating partners are required to sign an SAP FBA (Feedback Agreement).

If you are interested in joining PAC, please contact:

Bernie.xiong@sap.com

We will follow up with further details and invitations.

entry.title: SAP 公有云合作伙伴顾问委员会（PAC）— 销售模块
entry.content:
SAP 公有云合作伙伴顾问委员会（PAC）

让合作更直接，让产品更贴近客户

随着中国市场对公有云的监管环境逐步完善，企业上云的障碍正在逐渐减少。SAP 也在持续推进本地化布局，例如通过与阿里云开展战略合作，在中国提供符合合规要求的公有云解决方案。

与此同时，中国中小企业对订阅制 ERP 的接受度正在不断提升，越来越多企业希望通过云化实现更快部署与持续创新。

在这一趋势下，SAP 公有云生态也在不断扩展。除了传统的全球合作伙伴（如 IBM、PwC、德勤等），我们也看到越来越多本土合作伙伴加入到公有云实施与交付中来。

作为最贴近客户的一线伙伴，你们不仅了解客户的真实需求，也理解这些需求背后的业务场景与挑战。

基于这一背景，SAP Public Cloud Sales 推出了 SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council（PAC）。

为什么要设立 PAC？

在日常项目与销售过程中，我们持续听到合作伙伴的反馈：

  • 希望更早了解 SAP 的产品 roadmap
  • 希望更清楚哪些能力会交付、何时交付
  • 希望项目中的真实需求可以更直接进入产品讨论

过去，我们主要通过 WCR 和 CIP 等渠道收集反馈。

这些机制在结构化需求收集方面非常重要，但也存在一些局限：

  • 反馈周期较长，难以及时响应项目中的实际问题
  • 更多基于单点需求，缺少对完整业务场景的讨论
  • 需求如何进入产品 roadmap 的路径不够透明

因此，我们希望建立一种更加直接、高效的沟通方式。

👉 PAC 的目标是：让沟通更直接，让信息更透明，让反馈更有效，从而持续提升产品的可用性。

在 PAC 中，我们会做什么？

PAC 聚焦真实产品、真实场景和真实问题。

Roadmap 分享
分享 SAP Public Cloud（如 S/4HANA Cloud、BTP）的最新 roadmap，并说明对销售与项目交付的影响。

伙伴问题收集与讨论
提前收集项目中的问题，在会议中集中讨论，并明确后续跟进路径。

连接产品团队
邀请产品团队参与讨论，让合作伙伴需求更直接进入产品设计。

案例与经验分享
包括项目实践、集成与扩展场景，以及销售与交付经验。

我们已经讨论过哪些内容？

PAC 的讨论基于真实议程展开，例如：

  • SAP Cloud ERP for Sales 战略与 roadmap
  • Fiori roadmap（2602 / 2608）及系统演示
  • Manage Sales Documents 场景
  • Extensibility 与 UI 使用案例
  • Custom Sales Order Type
  • 伙伴成功案例与客户实践
  • 产品调研、用户研究及伙伴反馈

同时，每次会议都会安排时间用于开放讨论、问答以及明确下一步行动。

持续演进的机制

PAC 不是一次性的会议，而是一个持续优化的机制。

👉 我们会根据合作伙伴最关心的议题，持续设计下一次 PAC 的内容。

这意味着讨论始终贴近实际需求，反馈能够真正影响产品优化。

合作伙伴可以获得什么？

通过参与 PAC，合作伙伴可以：

  • 更早了解产品方向，支持销售决策
  • 更清楚能力落地情况，减少项目不确定性
  • 更直接推动需求进入产品讨论
  • 与其他伙伴交流经验

如何参与？

SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council 采用邀请制。

参与伙伴需要签署 SAP 的 FBA（Feedback Agreement），以支持深入的产品与需求交流。

如果您对参与 PAC 感兴趣，欢迎通过邮件联系我们：

📧 bernie.xiong@sap.com

我们将在确认后发送正式邀请及后续信息。

entry.title: [SAP Partners and Colleagues only] We've officially kicked off our Product Expert Training H1 2026
entry.content:
Discover our new format of the Product Expert Training (PET) and benefit from hands-on product expert insights, best practices, and the latest AI-powered innovations across SAP Business Suite. 

In H1 2026, expert insights will be delivered through regularly published on-demand expert videos, making it easier to access focused knowledge when you need it. 

In a live executive session, Florian Heretsch, Bert Schulze, and Corina Kinzel introduced the H1 2026 program and shared the shift from live sessions to on-demand, snackable product expert videos.

 

Watch the recording here.

 

New content available every Friday!

From March 20–June 26, we’re releasing new product expert videos covering end‑to‑end processes, innovations, features, and practical project insights across the entire SAP Business Suite. 

We've published several videos covering the following topics:

  • Finance and risk
  • Source to pay
  • Plan to fulfill
  • Acquire to decommission
  • Lead to cash
  • Cross topics

These expert videos (5–30 minutes) covering product insights, new features and innovations, and implementation experiences will be published weekly on SAP Learning until the end of June. Partners will receive regular updates with links to the newly published videos.

Check out the first batch of videos and stay tuned for more videos to follow every Friday*!

*Please note that, due to public holidays, no new videos will be released on April 3 and May 1.

 

Check out our landing page for more information.

 

Looking ahead: PET live event in H2 2026

In the second half of 2026, PET will culminate in a flagship hybrid event starting on September 28 and running for approximately three weeks, bringing together the partner community and SAP product experts.


The event will combine live sessions and networking opportunities, with the possibility to welcome selected partners onsite while also enabling virtual participation.

More details about the H2 flagship event will follow soon.

entry.title: How SAP Situation Handling Automates Issue Detection & Empowers Your Team?
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, critical issues—like an expiring contract or an un-billed sales order—can slip through the cracks, leading to revenue loss, added costs, and operational delays. What if your Cloud ERP system could automatically find these problems and notify the right person at the right time?

In this session replay from our AI Readiness Service, Knut Manske , Engineering Lead for SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP Foundation, demystifies SAP Situation Handling. He moves beyond theory to show you exactly how this powerful, license-included framework works to transform reactive firefighting into proactive business management. 

Core Value Proposition: The "Why" of Situation Handling

Situation Handling is designed to recognize issues requiring attention at the right moment and provide the right information to the right person.​ It acts as an automated watchdog for your business processes, eliminating the need for users to manually sift through reports and lists to find problems.

Key Benefits Highlighted in the Session:
  • Proactive, Not Reactive:  The system automatically detects issues in the background (e.g., budgets exceeded, contracts expiring, confirmations pending) before they cause damage.
  • Context-Aware Notifications: Responsible users are informed via SAP Fiori notifications, email, SAP Mobile Start, and even within Microsoft Teams, with direct links to resolve the issue.
  • Unified View for Faster Resolution: A dedicated "Situation Page" consolidates all relevant data from different sources, giving users the full context they need to act immediately.
  • Low-Code Flexibility: Start fast with 140+ pre-delivered use cases, or use the low-code, extended framework to build and customize your own scenarios without extensive development.
  • Foundation for AI: This is the core infrastructure that enables upcoming AI-driven recommendations, where the system can suggest actions based on company guidelines (planned for 2026).
 
See it in Action: Main Use Cases Demonstrated
  • The Everyday Overview: See All Your Issues in One Place (Video Timestamp: 18:54 - 20:58)
  • From Alert to Action in One Click (Video Timestamp: 22:02 - 22:55)
  • Activate a Pre-Built Use Case in Minutes (Video Timestamp: 30:31 - 38:47)
  • Deep Dive: Finance & GR/IR Reconciliation (Video Timestamp: 39:40 - 44:15)

Watch the full session replay​ to get the complete picture, including the live Q&A where Knut and product manager Ankitkumar Kaneri  answer specific questions. SAP Partners and Employee could access SAP Partner Portal for session replay and slides access.

entry.title: Custom Business Configurations (F4579): Define default grouping and sorting
entry.content:
Introduction

The Custom Business Configurations (CUBCO) app serves as an entry point to the Business Configuration Maintenance Object (SMBC) provided by custom applications or partners.

This blog explains how to define the default grouping and sorting for the tables of the business configuration object.
The basic procedure is already explained here, but we apply it directly to the result of the ADT wizard.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Example scenario

Company codes are defined in the configuration table ZDEMO_CC. In the CUBCO app, the rows are to be grouped and sorted by country.

@EndUserText.label : 'Company Code'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zdemo_cc {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key company_code      : abap.numc(4) not null;
  country               : land1;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

Define presentation variant

First you use the ADT wizard to generate a Business Configuration Maintenance Object for this table.

In the generated CDS view ZI_COMPANYCODE_S of the Singleton entity, you change the facet type from #LINEITEM_REFERENCE to #PRESENTATIONVARIANT_REFERENCE and add the targetQualifier attribute:

  @ui.facet: [ {
    id: 'ZI_CompanyCode', 
    purpose: #STANDARD, 
    type: #PRESENTATIONVARIANT_REFERENCE, 
    targetQualifier: 'myVariant',
    label: 'Company Codes', 
    position: 1 , 
    targetElement: '_CompanyCode'
  } ]
  .lineItem: [ {
    position: 1 
  } ]
  key 1 as SingletonID,

In the metadata extension ZI_COMPANYCODE of the company code entity, add the following presentation variant:

@ui.presentationVariant: [
  {
    qualifier: 'myVariant',
    sortOrder: [
      {
        by: 'Country',
        direction: #ASC
      }
    ],
    groupBy: [ 'Country' ],
    visualizations: [{type: #AS_LINEITEM}]
  }
]
annotate view ZI_CompanyCode with

Finally, open the generated SMBC object ZCOMPANYCODE in ADT and open the table settings section. Change the table type attribute from Grid Table to Responsive Table because grouping is not supported for grid tables. If you only need the default sorting, you can continue to use the grid table.

entry.title: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the S/4HANA Public Edition (Pt D: Improvements)
entry.content:
Introduction

Now Artificial Intelligence (AI) is on every professional’s mind. Everybody wants to be on the bandwagon.  I plan to write a series of blogs on this topic under a big umbrella “My AI Experience Sharing”.  This is the first mini-series: setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

I purposely delayed my action on setting up a Joule to wait for the dust settling down during the Early Adopter phase. The intention paid off but still won’t give me a free pass 😉. There are five credible references available as listed in the Reference section, but none of them give you a good explanation on Why, especially on What in a critical step with a readable picture when you desperately need a hint.  To help all the people like me, this blog will focus on explaining those steps, what to enter, and possible mistakes to avoid. 

This week, I re-did the Joule Setup on a Parallel Line of the SAP S/4HANA ERP Public Edition, aka Public Cloud. I noticed a few changes and would like to outline here for your reference:

You can install Joule on your Parallel Line

Several weeks ago, I met one of our customers at the Customer Success Day in NYC. After listening to his rational behind requiring Joule on the Parallel Line, I promised to do a test. I did just that this week on a Parallel Line. It worked.

No need to NavigationService

I heard there is an improvement in the Setup process, i.e., no more need to manually create the NavigationService destination (outlined in Step B.2.4 of my blog My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the S/4HANA Public Edition (Pt B:Joule Installation). If you continue to add this destination, it won’t hurt because the system is no longer seeking the information stored there. I will remove this step in my blog.

Joule Booster is Faster

The Joule Booster is noticeably faster.  In my case, it is about 15 minutes to complete the running.

SAP Work Zone Content Provider

When I provision the user and group information from the Public Cloud to the Work Zone via IPS, I got an error: The provided providerId is illegal: "status":400,"scimType":"invalidValue".  There is even an KBA addressing this problem 3505749 - The provided providerId is illegal: "status":400,"scimType":"invalidValue".  It turns out not because of my ProviderID is bad, but I only copied part of the ProviderID 6d5e30cb-4 from sap.start.6d5e30cb-4.  I learned that from creating my own ProviderID.  The entire string is the ProviderID, not part of it.  The details count.

References

  1. Blog 1: Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
  2. Blog 2: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  3. Discovery Center 1: Establish a Unified Joule Instance
  4. Discovery Center 2: Activate Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  5. SAP Help: Joule onboarding guide
  6. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part A Prepara...
  7. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part B Joule I...
  8. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part C Joule D...
  9. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part D Improvements)

entry.title: Measuring Business Outcomes in SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
This blog's intent is to expand on my previous one: Sample value drivers for effective Business Value Realization of your Public Cloud Solution.

Now that you have identified the value drivers and specific solution capabilities that can bring you additional business value, how do you go about knowing if you are improving? How do you go about measuring the value of your improvements without creating a sophisticated value driver baseline or measuring the value drivers in a qualitative way? In this blog, I also want to provide you with a simple and pragmatic approach using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition standard applications as a source to get some information to establish the baseline (you might still need to do some calculations if you want to measure the exact value driver), but by using standard apps you can use them as an alternative to see if your business is improving, which, in the end, without a lot of effort, is what you want to do—measure improvement.  Obviously, if you want real value driver measurement you can also play with analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to either create your own KPI, reports, multidimensional reports, stories, or booklets, to make an exact calculation of the value driver.

Remember, the approach I am suggesting here is not the only approach, as SAP has other services like Value Management and/or other tools like SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, etc., that can help you with this exercise as well. In my previous blog, I covered very briefly how SAP Signavio Process Intelligence — Drive more business outcomes out of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – A Recommendation Guide

Now let's recap on the different areas we suggested in my previous blog:

  • Reduce selling, general, and administrative expenses
  • Increase revenue
  • Decrease cost of goods sold (COGS)
  • Improve working capital
  • Improve pre-tax margin

Sample value drivers by improvement area

Selling, general, and administrative expenses

Ok, let’s start with lowering our selling, general, and administrative expenses. Here I recommended the following value drivers in my last blog: “Reduce account receivable management costs” and “Reduce invoice error rate.”

Reduce account receivable management costs – This value driver measures the total cost of accounts receivable as a percentage of revenue, so yes, you can use the system and create some specific analytics that can collect several general ledger accounts for your accounts receivable and then compare with your revenue to calculate this. So, somehow this value driver requires some information and calculation to come up with the total cost of the accounts receivable function and compare with your revenue. But my advice is that you instead look to see improvements metrics on accounts receivable to measure the real impact on improvements out of the box and not only the cost. For that, I recommend you use the Accounts Receivable Overview App, which will provide you with other KPIs to improve your AR function, like AR aging analysis, Top 10 Debtors, Exceeded Credit Limit, etc. This will provide you a meaningful measurement out of the box to measure improvements you can easily compare every time you use this app.

Accounts Receivables Overview

For the Reduce invoice error rate, as we are focusing on Accounts Receivable, I suggest you use the Overdue Receivables in Dispute and select some of the reasons like “Price Difference.” While this doesn’t give you an automatic error rate, you can use disputed amounts with the price difference reason, for example, to set the baseline to compare. Otherwise, you will need to create specific analytics that calculate all invoices and allow you to look at dispute cases over a specific time period, which complicates the measurement, unless you want to run it a bit more qualitatively.

Overdue Receivables in Dispute – filter by Reason: Price Difference

Revenue

On Revenue, we selected two different value drivers: Reduce manufacturing cycle time and Reduce revenue loss due to fulfillment issues.

Here I am recommending two apps that can provide you with this information. For reducing manufacturing cycle time, you can use the Production Execution Duration app. This app will be able to provide you with the plan and actual information by work center, which you can use to measure your improvements. You can then filter this by material, planning plant, production plan, and work center.

Production Execution Duration

Now for the Reduce revenue loss due to fulfillment issues, you can use the Sales Order Fulfillment – Analyze and Resolve app.

This app will help you look at current fulfillment issues and try to resolve them.

Sales Order Fulfillment – Analyze and Resolve

Cost of Good Sold

For the two selected value drivers, Reduce total manufacturing costs and Reduce asset maintenance costs, we have two apps that we suggest to use. The first one is the Analyze Production Costs – Event Based app, to help you measure the total manufacturing cost. There might be other apps you may want to use depending on your specific production process.

Production Costs – Event Based

The second one to help measure the reduction of maintenance costs is the app, Maintenance Order Costs Plan and Actuals. This app will help you to execute these tasks.

Maintenance Oder Costs – Plan/Actuals

Working Capital

In working capital, we suggested two value drivers: reduce sales days outstanding and reduce days in inventory.

To measure these two value drivers, you can use the following applications. For sales days outstanding, you can use the app “Days Sales Outstanding – 12 months,” which can provide you with a quick way to measure this value driver. The app provides you with the option to see this by period, by customer, and by company code.

Days Sales Outstanding – 12 months

The app Inventory Turnover Analysis can provide you with the reduction of days in inventory.

Inventory Turnover Analysis

Pre-tax Margin

In the pre-tax margin area, we selected two value drivers: improve on-time delivery performance and reduce uncollectible accounts receivable write-offs.

We could use the Delivery Performance app to help improve on-time delivery performance. We have two apps to choose from: Delivery Performance as Requested and Delivery Performance as Committed. Both apps look similar; the difference is one presents the information as requested and the other as committed. We also have an app that helps you analyze delivery performance – shipped as planned.

Delivery Performance as Requested

And to reduce uncollectible accounts receivable write-offs, we suggest using the app Cash Collector Tracker – Account Receivables. The app allows you to see this by Company Code, by Accounting Clerk, and by top 10 customers.

Collector Tracker – Account Receivables

I hope these out-of-the-box apps provide you an alternative to complex calculations to identify measurements for your value drivers as you drive improvements to your business.

I also want to thank my colleagues Edna Garcia and Jens Klawiter from Cloud ERP Product Success for the joint discussions on this topic and the checks we executed together in SAP Cloud ERP.

Keep in mind that solution capabilities are expanding from traditional functional scope to also include SAP Business AI, so SAP will continue to add more in this area. As in my previous blogs, remember, this is not the only tool that can be used; SAP has other tools such as SAP Signavio, SAP WalkMe, SAP LeanIX, and SAP Cloud ALM that can be used in tandem to help drive more business value from your SAP subscription.

I encourage you to discuss these points with your SAP Customer Success Manager, SAP Account Executive, or SAP Partner. They may be able to provide you with additional recommendations and guidance.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by commenting on this blog.

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia (SAP Business Suite Customer Success Management).
